### Install Dependencies and Initialize TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/webassembly-wasi.md

Commands to install project dependencies and initialize TypeScript configuration after the initial project setup.

```sh
npm i
npx tsc --init
```

```sh
yarn
yarn tsc --init
```

```sh
pnpm i
pnpm exec tsc --init
```

```sh
bun i
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Swagger UI Setup with Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/swagger-ui.md

This example demonstrates how to integrate Swagger UI into a Hono application, serving an OpenAPI document and making it accessible via a UI endpoint.

```TypeScript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { swaggerUI } from '@hono/swagger-ui'

// A basic OpenAPI document
const openApiDoc = {
  openapi: '3.0.0', // This is the required version field
  info: {
    title: 'API Documentation',
    version: '1.0.0',
    description: 'API documentation for your service',
  },
  paths: {
    // Add your API paths here
    '/health': {
      get: {
        summary: 'Health check',
        responses: {
          '200': {
            description: 'OK',
          },
        },
      },
    },
    // Add more endpoints as needed
  },
}

const app = new Hono()

// Serve the OpenAPI document
app.get('/doc', (c) => c.json(openApiDoc))

// Use the middleware to serve Swagger UI at /ui
app.get('/ui', swaggerUI({ url: '/doc' }))

app.get('/health', (c) => c.text('OK'))

export default app
```

--------------------------------

### Hello World server in Deno

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/deno.md

Basic Hono application that responds with 'Hello Deno!' on GET /. Uses `Deno.serve` to start the server.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello Deno!'))

Deno.serve(app.fetch)
```

--------------------------------

### Navigate and install dependencies for a Bun project

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/bun.md

After creating a new project, change into the project directory and install its dependencies.

```sh
cd my-app
bun install
```

--------------------------------

### Full Server and Client WebSocket Example

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/websocket.md

Demonstrates a complete WebSocket setup with a Hono server using Cloudflare Workers helper and a client sending messages.

```ts
// server.ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { upgradeWebSocket } from 'hono/cloudflare-workers'

const app = new Hono().get(
  '/ws',
  upgradeWebSocket(() => {
    return {
      onMessage: (event) => {
        console.log(event.data)
      },
    }
  })
)

export default app
```

```ts
// client.ts
import { hc } from 'hono/client'
import type app from './server'

const client = hc<typeof app>('http://localhost:8787')
const ws = client.ws.$ws(0)

ws.addEventListener('open', () => {
  setInterval(() => {
    ws.send(new Date().toString())
  }, 1000)
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/nextjs.md

Navigate to the project directory and install required dependencies using your package manager.

```sh
cd my-app
npm i
```

```sh
cd my-app
yarn
```

```sh
cd my-app
pnpm i
```

```sh
cd my-app
bun i
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies after project creation

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/basic.md

Navigate to the project directory and install dependencies using your package manager of choice.

```sh
cd my-app
npm i
```

```sh
cd my-app
yarn
```

```sh
cd my-app
pnpm i
```

```sh
cd my-app
bun i
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies with bun

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/service-worker.md

Install Hono and Vite as development dependencies using bun.

```sh
bun add hono
bun add -D vite
```

--------------------------------

### Start development server with bun

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/service-worker.md

Run the Vite development server using bun.

```sh
bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### GET / (Using ConnInfo Helper)

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/conninfo.md

This example demonstrates how to integrate the `getConnInfo` helper within a Hono GET route to retrieve and display the client's remote IP address.

```APIDOC
## GET /

### Description
Retrieves the client's remote IP address using the `getConnInfo` helper function and returns it as plain text. This demonstrates how to access connection-specific details within a Hono route.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **text** (string) - The client's remote IP address, formatted as "Your remote address is [IP_ADDRESS]".

#### Response Example
Your remote address is 192.168.1.1
```

--------------------------------

### Install Better Auth Framework

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/better-auth.md

Install the Better Auth framework using various package managers.

```sh
# npm
npm install better-auth

# bun
bun add better-auth

# pnpm
pnpm add better-auth

# yarn
yarn add better-auth
```

--------------------------------

### Install Stripe SDK

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/stripe-webhook.md

Install the official Stripe SDK to interact with Stripe APIs and handle webhooks.

```bash
npm install stripe
```

### Install Prisma CLI

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/prisma.md

Install Prisma as a development dependency in your Hono project.

```bash
npm i prisma --save-dev
```

### Install Stytch and Hono Auth Libraries

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/stytch-auth.md

Install the necessary backend and frontend packages for Stytch authentication with Hono using various package managers.

```sh
# Backend
npm install @hono/stytch-auth stytch

# Frontend
npm install @stytch/react @stytch/vanilla-js
```

```sh
# Backend
yarn add @hono/stytch-auth stytch

# Frontend
yarn add @stytch/react @stytch/vanilla-js
```

```sh
# Backend
pnpm add @hono/stytch-auth stytch

# Frontend
pnpm add @stytch/react @stytch/vanilla-js
```

```sh
# Backend
bun add @hono/stytch-auth stytch

# Frontend
bun add @stytch/react @stytch/vanilla-js
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Hono project with bun

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/ali-function-compute.md

Initialize a new Hono project with required dependencies and directory structure using bun.

```sh
mkdir my-app
cd my-app
bun add hono hono-alibaba-cloud-fc3-adapter
bun add -D esbuild @serverless-devs/s
mkdir src
touch src/index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Navigate and Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/google-cloud-run.md

Changes the directory to the new Hono project and installs its npm dependencies.

```sh
cd my-app
npm i
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Deno project with Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/deno.md

Create a new Deno project with Hono using the starter template. No explicit Hono installation is required.

```sh
deno init --npm hono --template=deno my-app
```

```bash

```bash

```

```bash

```

```bash
bun add apitally

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/dev.md

```text
GET   /v1/posts
GET   /v1/posts/:id
POST  /v1/posts
```

### Quick Start: Create Hono Project

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/index.md

Use these commands to quickly scaffold a new Hono project with your preferred package manager or runtime.

```sh
npm create hono@latest
```

```sh
yarn create hono
```

```sh
pnpm create hono@latest
```

```sh
bun create hono@latest
```

```sh
deno init --npm hono@latest
```

```sh

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/nextjs.md

```sh
npm run dev

```

bun run dev

--------------------------------

### Language Detection Client Examples

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/middleware/builtin/language.md

Examples of how clients can specify language preference via path, query parameter, cookie, or Accept-Language header.

```shell
# Via path
curl http://localhost:8787/ar/home

# Via query parameter
curl http://localhost:8787/?lang=ar

# Via cookie
curl -H 'Cookie: language=ja' http://localhost:8787/

# Via header
curl -H 'Accept-Language: ar,en;q=0.9' http://localhost:8787/
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/service-worker.md

Install Hono and Vite as development dependencies using pnpm.

```sh
pnpm add hono
pnpm add -D vite
```

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/service-worker.md

Install Hono and Vite as development dependencies using npm.

```sh
npm i hono
npm i -D vite
```

Install Valibot and its JSON schema converter for use as the validation library.

npm install prisma --save-dev

npm install @prisma/client
npm install @prisma/adapter-d1

### Create a Basic Hono GET Endpoint

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/concepts/stacks.md

Demonstrates how to set up a simple GET endpoint in Hono that returns a JSON response.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()

app.get('/hello', (c) => {
  return c.json({
    message: `Hello!`,
  })
})
```

netlify dev

```sh
npm run dev
```

```sh
yarn dev
```

```sh
pnpm dev
```

```sh
bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Hono Web API Example

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/web-api.md

This example demonstrates a complete Hono application for a blog API, including routing, middleware for CORS and basic authentication, and CRUD operations for posts.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { cors } from 'hono/cors'
import { basicAuth } from 'hono/basic-auth'
import { prettyJSON } from 'hono/pretty-json'
import { getPosts, getPost, createPost, Post } from './model'

const app = new Hono()
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Pretty Blog API'))
app.use(prettyJSON())
app.notFound((c) => c.json({ message: 'Not Found', ok: false }, 404))

type Bindings = {
  USERNAME: string
  PASSWORD: string
}

const api = new Hono<{ Bindings: Bindings }>()
api.use('/posts/*', cors())

api.get('/posts', (c) => {
  const { limit, offset } = c.req.query()
  const posts = getPosts({ limit, offset })
  return c.json({ posts })
})

api.get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
  const id = c.req.param('id')
  const post = getPost({ id })
  return c.json({ post })
})

api.post(
  '/posts',
  async (c, next) => {
    const auth = basicAuth({
      username: c.env.USERNAME,
      password: c.env.PASSWORD,
    })
    return auth(c, next)
  },
  async (c) => {
    const post = await c.req.json<Post>()
    const ok = createPost({ post })
    return c.json({ ok })
  }
)

app.route('/api', api)

export default app
```

--------------------------------

### Run a Hono application with Bun

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/bun.md

Execute the 'dev' script defined in `package.json` to start the Hono application.

```sh
bun run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Hono and Azure Functions Adapter

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/azure-functions.md

Install the Hono framework and the Azure Functions Adapter package for integrating Hono with Azure Functions.

```npm
npm i @marplex/hono-azurefunc-adapter hono
```

```yarn
yarn add @marplex/hono-azurefunc-adapter hono
```

```pnpm
pnpm add @marplex/hono-azurefunc-adapter hono
```

```bun
bun add @marplex/hono-azurefunc-adapter hono
```

--------------------------------

### Start Supabase Stack and Functions Watcher

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/supabase-functions.md

Start the Supabase stack and the Functions watcher for local development. The --no-verify-jwt flag bypasses JWT verification during development.

```bash
supabase start # start the supabase stack
supabase functions serve --no-verify-jwt # start the Functions watcher
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize App with initApp Option

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/factory.md

Configure `createFactory()` with an `initApp` option to apply common middleware or setup to all applications created by `factory.createApp()`.

```ts
// factory-with-db.ts
type Env = {
  Bindings: {
    MY_DB: D1Database
  }
  Variables: {
    db: DrizzleD1Database
  }
}

export default createFactory<Env>({
  initApp: (app) => {
    app.use(async (c, next) => {
      const db = drizzle(c.env.MY_DB)
      c.set('db', db)
      await next()
    })
  }
})
```

```ts
// crud.ts
import factoryWithDB from './factory-with-db'

const app = factoryWithDB.createApp()

app.post('/posts', (c) => {
  c.var.db.insert()
  // ...
})
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Pretty JSON Middleware

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/middleware/builtin/pretty-json.md

Initialize a Hono app and apply the prettyJSON middleware. Optionally configure indentation with the space option.

```typescript
const app = new Hono()

app.use(prettyJSON()) // With options: prettyJSON({ space: 4 })
app.get('/', (c) => {
  return c.json({ message: 'Hono!' })
})
```

--------------------------------

### Define REST API endpoints in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/guides/testing.md

Example Hono application with GET and POST endpoints returning text and JSON responses with custom headers.

```typescript
app.get('/posts', (c) => {
  return c.text('Many posts')
})

app.post('/posts', (c) => {
  return c.json(
    {
      message: 'Created',
    },
    201,
    {
      'X-Custom': 'Thank you',
    }
  )
})
```

--------------------------------

### Run Deno development server

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/deno.md

Start the development server using the task runner. Accesses the application at http://localhost:8000 by default.

```sh
deno task start
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies for Hono, Better Auth, Drizzle, and Neon

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/better-auth-on-cloudflare.md

Install required packages for Hono, Better Auth, Drizzle ORM, and Neon database connector using various package managers.

```sh
# Hono
# > Select cloudflare-workers template
npm create hono

# Better Auth
npm install better-auth

# Drizzle ORM
npm install drizzle-orm
npm install --save-dev drizzle-kit

# Neon
npm install @neondatabase/serverless
```

```sh
# Hono
# > Select cloudflare-workers template
pnpm create hono

# Better Auth
pnpm add better-auth

# Drizzle ORM
pnpm add drizzle-orm
pnpm add -D drizzle-kit

# Neon
pnpm add @neondatabase/serverless
```

```sh
# Hono
# > Select cloudflare-workers template
yarn create hono

# Better Auth
yarn add better-auth

# Drizzle ORM
yarn add drizzle-orm
yarn add --dev drizzle-kit

# Neon
yarn add @neondatabase/serverless
```

```sh
# Hono
# > Select cloudflare-workers template
bun create hono

# Better Auth
bun add better-auth

# Drizzle ORM
bun add drizzle-orm
bun add -d drizzle-kit

# Neon
bun add @neondatabase/serverless
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Pylon project with npm

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/pylon.md

Use this command to initialize a new Pylon project with a basic structure. The example uses the Bun runtime.

```bash
npm create pylon my-pylon@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install typed-htmx package

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/htmx.md

Install the typed-htmx package as a development dependency.

```sh
npm i -D typed-htmx
```

--------------------------------

### Handle HEAD requests with a GET route

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/guides/best-practices.md

Define a GET route to automatically handle HEAD requests, as Hono converts HEAD to GET and strips the response body by default.

```typescript
// GOOD: This GET route automatically handles HEAD requests
app.get('/api/users', async (c) => {
  const users = await getUsers()
  c.header('X-Total-Count', users.length.toString())
  return c.json(users)
})

// HEAD /api/users will return:
// - Same headers as GET (including X-Total-Count)
// - Status 200
// - No body (null)
```

--------------------------------

### Run Hono Development Server

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/google-cloud-run.md

Starts the Hono application in development mode, typically accessible on localhost.

```sh
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Supabase Project

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/supabase-functions.md

Initialize a new Supabase project in the current directory. Run this command once to set up the project structure.

```bash
supabase init
```

--------------------------------

### Hello World Server using Web Standards (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/concepts/web-standard.md

This example shows a minimal server implementation that returns 'Hello World' using Web Standards, demonstrating compatibility with runtimes like Cloudflare Workers and Bun.

```typescript
export default {
  async fetch() {
    return new Response('Hello World')
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Import Hono Factory Helpers

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/factory.md

Import necessary functions from 'hono' and 'hono/factory' to start using the helper utilities.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { createFactory, createMiddleware } from 'hono/factory'
```

### Install Zod Validator Middleware

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/guides/validation.md

Install the `@hono/zod-validator` package using your preferred package manager.

```sh
npm i @hono/zod-validator
```

```sh
yarn add @hono/zod-validator
```

```sh
pnpm add @hono/zod-validator
```

```sh
bun add @hono/zod-validator
```

--------------------------------

### Expected Output from jco serve

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/webassembly-wasi.md

This is the console output displayed when `jco serve` successfully starts the WebAssembly HTTP server.

```sh
$ npx jco serve dist/component.wasm
Server listening @ localhost:8000...
```

--------------------------------

### Hello World application with fire() adapter

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/fastly.md

Basic Hono application for Fastly Compute using the fire() adapter from @fastly/hono-fastly-compute. Use Hono from 'hono' (not 'hono/quick') when fire() is at the top level, as it builds the router during initialization.

```ts
// src/index.ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
import { fire } from '@fastly/hono-fastly-compute'

const app = new Hono()

app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello Fastly!'))

fire(app)
```

--------------------------------

### Creating a Standard Web API Response Object

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/context.md

This example shows how to create a standard `Response` object, which is equivalent to what `c.body()` can produce.

```ts
new Response('Thank you for coming', {
  status: 201,
  headers: {
    'X-Message': 'Hello!',
    'Content-Type': 'text/plain'
  }
})
```

--------------------------------

### Encrypted HTTP/2 Server Setup

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/nodejs.md

Create an encrypted HTTP/2 server with TLS certificates. Requires key and certificate files in PEM format.

```typescript
import { createSecureServer } from 'node:http2'
import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'

const server = serve({
  fetch: app.fetch,
  createServer: createSecureServer,
  serverOptions: {
    key: readFileSync('localhost-privkey.pem'),
    cert: readFileSync('localhost-cert.pem'),
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Hono project with Bun

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/bun.md

Use this command to initialize a new Hono project using the official Hono Bun starter template.

```sh
bun create hono@latest my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Install ArkType for Standard Schema Validation

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/guides/validation.md

Install the `arktype` package to use it as a validation library with `@hono/standard-validator`.

```sh
npm i arktype
```

```sh
yarn add arktype
```

```sh
pnpm add arktype
```

```sh
bun add arktype
```

--------------------------------

### Install Deno standard library assertion module

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/deno.md

Add the `@std/assert` module from JSR for writing test assertions. Required before importing assertion functions.

```sh
deno add jsr:@std/assert
```

--------------------------------

### Install Node.js adapter for Pages Router

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/nextjs.md

Install the @hono/node-server adapter required to use Hono with Next.js Pages Router.

```sh
npm i @hono/node-server
```

```sh
yarn add @hono/node-server
```

```sh
pnpm add @hono/node-server
```

```sh
bun add @hono/node-server
```

--------------------------------

### Create Hono project for Fastly Compute

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/fastly.md

Initialize a new Hono project with the fastly template using create-hono. Choose your preferred package manager.

```sh
npm create hono@latest my-app
```

```sh
yarn create hono my-app
```

```sh
pnpm create hono my-app
```

```sh
bun create hono@latest my-app
```

```sh
deno init --npm hono my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Hono 'Hello World' Application

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/index.md

This snippet shows how to initialize a Hono application and define a root GET route that responds with 'Hello Hono!' using Web Standard APIs.

```javascript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello Hono!'))

export default app
```

--------------------------------

### Install Cloudflare Workers types

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/cloudflare-workers.md

Install @cloudflare/workers-types as a dev dependency to enable TypeScript type support for Cloudflare Workers APIs.

```sh
npm i --save-dev @cloudflare/workers-types
```

```sh
yarn add -D @cloudflare/workers-types
```

```sh
pnpm add -D @cloudflare/workers-types
```

```sh
bun add --dev @cloudflare/workers-types
```

--------------------------------

### method - Get Request HTTP Method

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Returns the HTTP method name of the request (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.).

```APIDOC
## method

### Description
The method name of the request.

### Method
N/A (Property)

### Request Example
```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()

app.get('/about/me', async (c) => {
  const method = c.req.method // `GET`
  // ...
})
```

### Response
- **method** (string) - The HTTP method name (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
```

--------------------------------

### PatternRouter Bundle Size Example

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/concepts/routers.md

Console output demonstrating the minimal bundle size achieved when using only PatternRouter. An application with PatternRouter is under 15KB uncompressed and 5.38KB gzipped.

```console
$ npx wrangler deploy --minify ./src/index.ts
 ⛅️ wrangler 3.20.0
-------------------
Total Upload: 14.68 KiB / gzip: 5.38 KiB
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Hono Route for IDE Performance Example

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/guides/rpc.md

A simple Hono route illustrating how complex type instantiations can occur, potentially affecting IDE performance.

```ts
// app.ts
export const app = new Hono().get('foo/:id', (c) =>
  c.json({ ok: true }, 200)
)
```

--------------------------------

### Test GET request with app.request

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/guides/testing.md

Basic test for a GET endpoint using app.request to validate status code and response text.

```typescript
describe('Example', () => {
  test('GET /posts', async () => {
    const res = await app.request('/posts')
    expect(res.status).toBe(200)
    expect(await res.text()).toBe('Many posts')
  })
})
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Hono project with CDK and package manager

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/aws-lambda.md

Set up a new Hono project with AWS CDK, TypeScript, and esbuild. Choose the appropriate package manager (npm, yarn, pnpm, or bun).

```sh
mkdir my-app
cd my-app
cdk init app -l typescript
npm i hono
npm i -D esbuild
mkdir lambda
touch lambda/index.ts
```

```sh
mkdir my-app
cd my-app
cdk init app -l typescript
yarn add hono
yarn add -D esbuild
mkdir lambda
touch lambda/index.ts
```

```sh
mkdir my-app
cd my-app
cdk init app -l typescript
pnpm add hono
pnpm add -D esbuild
mkdir lambda
touch lambda/index.ts
```

```sh
mkdir my-app
cd my-app
cdk init app -l typescript
bun add hono
bun add -D esbuild
mkdir lambda
touch lambda/index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Install Zod for Hono Validation

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/guides/validation.md

Install the Zod library using various package managers to enable schema-based validation in Hono applications.

```sh
npm i zod
```

```sh
yarn add zod
```

```sh
pnpm add zod
```

```sh
bun add zod
```

--------------------------------

### Test GET request with app.request()

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/hono.md

Use app.request() to send a test GET request by passing a pathname. Returns a Response object for assertions.

```typescript
test('GET /hello is ok', async () => {
  const res = await app.request('/hello')
  expect(res.status).toBe(200)
})
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Multiple Hono SSG Plugins

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/ssg.md

This example demonstrates how to apply a combination of basic and advanced custom plugins, including the `sitemapPlugin`, to the `toSSG` function.

```ts
import app from './index'
import { toSSG } from 'hono/ssg'
import { sitemapPlugin } from './plugins'

toSSG(app, fs, {
  plugins: [
    getOnlyPlugin,
    statusFilterPlugin,
    logFilesPlugin,
    sitemapPlugin('https://example.com'),
  ],
})
```

--------------------------------

### Create Hono Project

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/google-cloud-run.md

Initializes a new Hono project named 'my-app' using the `create-hono` command.

```sh
npm create hono@latest my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Hono SSG Plugin to Filter GET Requests

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/ssg.md

This basic plugin demonstrates how to use `beforeRequestHook` to process only GET requests during static site generation.

```ts
const getOnlyPlugin: SSGPlugin = {
  beforeRequestHook: (req) => {
    if (req.method === 'GET') {
      return req
    }
    return false
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Azure Functions Core Tools on macOS

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/azure-functions.md

Install the Azure Functions Core Tools on macOS using Homebrew to manage and develop Azure Functions.

```sh
brew tap azure/functions
brew install azure-functions-core-tools@4
```

--------------------------------

### Create a Basic Hono Node.js Server

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/nodejs.md

Define a simple Hono application and serve it using `@hono/node-server`.

```ts
import { serve } from '@hono/node-server'
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello Node.js!'))

serve(app)
```

--------------------------------

### Get Full Request URL with c.req.url

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Use 'c.req.url' to get the complete URL string of the incoming request. This includes the protocol, host, path, and query parameters.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
// ---cut---
app.get('/about/me', async (c) => {
  const url = c.req.url // `http://localhost:8787/about/me`
  // ...
})
```

--------------------------------

### Hono Basic Routing with HTTP Methods and Wildcards

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/routing.md

Demonstrates how to define routes for standard HTTP methods, use wildcards, handle all methods, and specify custom methods or multiple paths.

```TypeScript
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
// ---cut---
// HTTP Methods
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('GET /'))
app.post('/', (c) => c.text('POST /'))
app.put('/', (c) => c.text('PUT /'))
app.delete('/', (c) => c.text('DELETE /'))

// Wildcard
app.get('/wild/*/card', (c) => {
  return c.text('GET /wild/*/card')
})

// Any HTTP methods
app.all('/hello', (c) => c.text('Any Method /hello'))

// Custom HTTP method
app.on('PURGE', '/cache', (c) => c.text('PURGE Method /cache'))

// Multiple Method
app.on(['PUT', 'DELETE'], '/post', (c) =>
  c.text('PUT or DELETE /post')
)

// Multiple Paths
app.on('GET', ['/hello', '/ja/hello', '/en/hello'], (c) =>
  c.text('Hello')
)
```

--------------------------------

### Get Request Method with c.req.method

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Access 'c.req.method' to retrieve the HTTP method (e.g., 'GET', 'POST') of the incoming request. This is useful for conditional logic based on the request type.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
// ---cut---
app.get('/about/me', async (c) => {
  const method = c.req.method // `GET`
  // ...
})
```

--------------------------------

### Handle POST and DELETE requests in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/basic.md

Define handlers for POST and DELETE HTTP methods. POST example returns a 201 Created status, DELETE example uses a path parameter.

```ts
app.post('/posts', (c) => c.text('Created!', 201))
app.delete('/posts/:id', (c) =>
  c.text(`${c.req.param('id')} is deleted!`)
)
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Hono project for Lambda@Edge with bun

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/lambda-edge.md

Set up a new Hono project with CDK and TypeScript using bun. Creates project structure with lambda directory for handler code.

```sh
mkdir my-app
cd my-app
cdk init app -l typescript
bun add hono
mkdir lambda
```

--------------------------------

### Get request header value in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Access a specific request header by its name.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
// ---cut---
app.get('/', (c) => {
  const userAgent = c.req.header('User-Agent')
  //      ^?
  return c.text(`Your user agent is ${userAgent}`)
})
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Hono 'Hello World' application for Bun

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/bun.md

This script defines a simple Hono application that responds with 'Hello Bun!' on the root path. Place it in `src/index.ts` for the `bun run dev` command.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'

const app = new Hono()
app.get('/', (c) => c.text('Hello Bun!'))

export default app
```

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/nextjs.md

Initialize a new Hono project with the Next.js template using the create-hono command. Choose your preferred package manager.

```sh
npm create hono@latest my-app
```

```sh
yarn create hono my-app
```

```sh
pnpm create hono my-app
```

```sh
bun create hono@latest my-app
```

```sh
deno init --npm hono my-app
```

### Create project directory

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/service-worker.md

Initialize a new project directory for the Hono Service Worker application.

```sh
mkdir my-app
cd my-app
```

--------------------------------

### Add Hono to an existing Bun project

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/bun.md

Install the Hono dependency into an existing Bun project.

```sh
bun add hono
```

--------------------------------

### app.fetch() - Request Entry Point

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/hono.md

The main entry point for handling requests. Used as the fetch handler in Cloudflare Workers, Bun, and other serverless environments.

```APIDOC
## app.fetch() - Request Entry Point

### Description
The main entry point for handling requests. This method is used as the fetch handler in Cloudflare Workers, Bun, and other serverless environments.

### Method
GET/POST/etc (handles all HTTP methods)

### Signature
app.fetch(request, env, ctx)

### Parameters
- **request** (Request) - Required - The incoming HTTP request object.
- **env** (object) - Optional - Environment variables and bindings (Cloudflare Workers).
- **ctx** (ExecutionContext) - Optional - Execution context (Cloudflare Workers).

### Request Example - Cloudflare Workers
```ts
export default {
  fetch(request: Request, env: Env, ctx: ExecutionContext) {
    return app.fetch(request, env, ctx)
  },
}
```

### Request Example - Bun
```ts
export default {
  port: 3000,
  fetch: app.fetch,
}
```

### Response
Returns a Response object with the appropriate status code, headers, and body.
```

--------------------------------

### Get querystring parameters in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Retrieve individual querystring parameters or all parameters as an object from the request.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
// ---cut---
// Query params
app.get('/search', async (c) => {
  const query = c.req.query('q')
  //     ^?
})

// Get all params at once
app.get('/search', async (c) => {
  const { q, limit, offset } = c.req.query()
  //      ^?
})
```

--------------------------------

### Get path parameters in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Retrieve individual path parameters or all parameters as an object from the request.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
// ---cut---
// Captured params
app.get('/entry/:id', async (c) => {
  const id = c.req.param('id')
  //    ^?
  // ...
})

// Get all params at once
app.get('/entry/:id/comment/:commentId', async (c) => {
  const { id, commentId } = c.req.param()
  //      ^?
})
```

--------------------------------

### Serve precompressed static files

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/deno.md

Enable `precompressed` option to automatically serve `.br` (Brotli) or `.gz` (Gzip) compressed versions based on Accept-Encoding header. Prioritizes Brotli, then Zstd, then Gzip.

```ts
app.get(
  '/static/*',
  serveStatic({
    precompressed: true,
  })
)
```

--------------------------------

### Custom `package.json` Scripts for Development and Deployment (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/better-auth-on-cloudflare.md

Example `package.json` scripts for local development, Cloudflare deployment, type generation, and Better Auth schema generation.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "wrangler dev",
    "deploy": "pnpm run cf-gen-types && wrangler secret bulk .dev.vars.production && wrangler deploy --minify",
    "cf-gen-types": "wrangler types --env-interface CloudflareBindings",
    "better-auth-gen-schema": "pnpm dlx @better-auth/cli@latest generate --config ./better-auth.config.ts --output ./src/db/schema.ts"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Basic CSRF protection setup

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/middleware/builtin/csrf.md

Enable default CSRF validation that checks both Origin and Sec-Fetch-Site headers.

```typescript
const app = new Hono()

// Default: both origin and sec-fetch-site validation
app.use(csrf())
```

--------------------------------

### Create Better Auth Instance with Prisma Adapter

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/examples/better-auth.md

Initialize the Better Auth instance, configuring it with Prisma for database, trusted origins, email/password authentication, and social providers.

```ts
import { betterAuth } from 'better-auth'
import { prismaAdapter } from 'better-auth/adapters/prisma'

import prisma from '@/db/index'
import env from '@/env'

export const auth = betterAuth({
  database: prismaAdapter(prisma, {
    provider: 'postgresql',
  }),
  // Allow requests from the frontend development server
  trustedOrigins: ['http://localhost:5173'],
  emailAndPassword: {
    enabled: true,
  },
  socialProviders: {
    github: {
      clientId: env.GITHUB_CLIENT_ID,
      clientSecret: env.GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET,
    },
    google: {
      clientId: env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID,
      clientSecret: env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET,
    },
  },
})

export type AuthType = {
  user: typeof auth.$Infer.Session.user | null
  session: typeof auth.$Infer.Session.session | null
}
```

--------------------------------

### url - Get Request URL

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Returns the complete request URL string including protocol, host, and path.

```APIDOC
## url

### Description
The request url strings.

### Method
N/A (Property)

### Request Example
```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()

app.get('/about/me', async (c) => {
  const url = c.req.url // `http://localhost:8787/about/me`
  // ...
})
```

### Response
- **url** (string) - The complete request URL
```

--------------------------------

### Configure static file serving in wrangler.toml

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/cloudflare-workers.md

Enable static file serving by specifying the assets directory in wrangler.toml. Files in the public directory will be served at their relative paths.

```toml
assets = { directory = "public" }
```

--------------------------------

### path - Get Request Pathname

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Returns the pathname of the request URL, excluding the protocol, host, and query string.

```APIDOC
## path

### Description
The request pathname.

### Method
N/A (Property)

### Request Example
```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()

app.get('/about/me', async (c) => {
  const pathname = c.req.path // `/about/me`
  // ...
})
```

### Response
- **pathname** (string) - The request pathname
```

--------------------------------

### HonoRequest.param()

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Retrieves values of path parameters from the request URL. Can get a specific parameter by name or all parameters as an object.

```APIDOC
## METHOD HonoRequest.param()

### Description
Get the values of path parameters from the request URL.

### Method
Instance Method

### Context
Used within Hono route handlers via `c.req`.

### Parameters
#### Method Parameters
- **name** (string) - Optional - The name of the path parameter to retrieve. If omitted, an object containing all path parameters is returned.

### Return Value
- **value** (string | object) - The value of the specified path parameter, or an object containing all path parameters if no name is provided.

### Example Usage
```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()

// Captured params
app.get('/entry/:id', async (c) => {
  const id = c.req.param('id')
  // ...
})

// Get all params at once
app.get('/entry/:id/comment/:commentId', async (c) => {
  const { id, commentId } = c.req.param()
  // ...
})
```
```

--------------------------------

### Return JSON response in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/basic.md

Handle a GET request and return JSON data with the application/json content type.

```ts
app.get('/api/hello', (c) => {
  return c.json({
    ok: true,
    message: 'Hello Hono!',
  })
})
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Usage of Server-Timing Middleware in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/middleware/builtin/timing.md

Demonstrates how to apply the timing middleware, add custom metrics, and time asynchronous operations using startTime, endTime, and wrapTime.

```js
// Specify the variable types to infer the `c.get('metric')`:
type Variables = TimingVariables

const app = new Hono<{ Variables: Variables }>()

// add the middleware to your router
app.use(timing());

app.get('/', async (c) => {

  // add custom metrics
  setMetric(c, 'region', 'europe-west3')

  // add custom metrics with timing, must be in milliseconds
  setMetric(c, 'custom', 23.8, 'My custom Metric')

  // start a new timer
  startTime(c, 'db');
  const data = await db.findMany(...);

  // end the timer
  endTime(c, 'db');

  // ...or you can also just wrap a Promise using this function:
  const data = await wrapTime(c, 'db', db.findMany(...));

  return c.json({ response: data });
});
```

--------------------------------

### Get multiple querystring parameters in Hono

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/api/request.md

Retrieve multiple values for a single querystring parameter, such as `?tags=A&tags=B`.

```ts
import { Hono } from 'hono'
const app = new Hono()
// ---cut---
app.get('/search', async (c) => {
  // tags will be string[]
  const tags = c.req.queries('tags')
  //     ^?
  // ...
})
```

--------------------------------

### JSON Response With Pretty Print

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/middleware/builtin/pretty-json.md

Example of a prettified JSON response when the pretty query parameter is added to the URL.

```javascript
// GET /?pretty
{
  "project": {
    "name": "Hono",
    "repository": "https://github.com/honojs/hono"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Build Commands for Package Managers

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/getting-started/nodejs.md

Run the build script using npm, yarn, pnpm, or bun. Choose the command matching your project's package manager.

```shell
npm run build
```

```shell
yarn run build
```

```shell
pnpm run build
```

```shell
bun run build
```

--------------------------------

### Centralized Env Definition with factory.createApp() (Solution)

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/helpers/factory.md

Demonstrates how `createFactory()` and `createApp()` allow you to define the `Env` type in a single place, reducing redundancy.

```ts
import { createFactory } from 'hono/factory'

// ...

// Set the `Env` to `createFactory()`
const factory = createFactory<Env>()

const app = factory.createApp()

// factory also has `createMiddleware()`
const mw = factory.createMiddleware(async (c, next) => {
  await next()
})
```

--------------------------------

### JSON Response Without Pretty Print

Source: https://github.com/honojs/website/blob/main/docs/middleware/builtin/pretty-json.md

Example of a standard JSON response without the pretty query parameter.

```javascript
// GET /
{"project":{"name":"Hono","repository":"https://github.com/honojs/hono"}}
```
